ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic dumpling food product class (FOODON_03302648); no specific pork-and-cabbage boiled dumpling entry exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKP covers filled pasta/dumplings as a category; no specific Chinese boiled pork-cabbage dumpling code exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept for pork and cabbage boiled dumplings identified in the clinical terminology.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is 8 handmade dumplings, weighing approximately 160 g, as sold by Din Tai Fung Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Dumplings, pork and vegetable, boiled — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy / Foundation Foods composite; cross-referenced with NCC Food and Nutrient Database category for boiled pork dumplings
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact Din Tai Fung or Singapore-specific entry exists in HPB Foodo or USDA Branded for this frozen product. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central entries for boiled pork-and-vegetable dumplings (e.g., FDC ID 2103689 and related entries) and cross-referenced with published nutrient data for Chinese boiled dumplings (shui jiao) in peer-reviewed literature. Per-100g values reflect a typical handmade pork-and-cabbage boiled dumpling with a thin wheat-flour wrapper.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al.; supplemented by University of Sydney GI Database entry for boiled wheat-flour dumplings / filled pasta
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for Din Tai Fung pork-and-cabbage dumplings exists. GI of 48 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2021) data for boiled wheat-flour filled dumplings and filled pasta products (GI range 39–55, mean ~48). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (48 × 36.0) / 100 = 17.3, where carbohydrate per 160 g serving = 22.5 × 1.6 = 36.0 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~420 mg per 100 g; ~672 mg per 8-piece serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to 4–6 pieces and avoid high-sodium dipping sauces such as soy sauce to keep total sodium intake within the recommended 2000 mg/day ceiling.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (~17 per 8-piece serving) from wheat-flour wrapper carbohydrates
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes can include a moderate portion (4–6 pieces) as part of a balanced meal; pairing with non-starchy vegetables and avoiding sweet dipping sauces will help attenuate postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~2.4 g per 100 g) and sodium (~420 mg per 100 g) from pork filling
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals managing cardiovascular disease should limit intake to one serving (8 pieces) and choose steaming or boiling over pan-frying to avoid additional fat, while monitoring cumulative sodium from sauces.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Handmade Pork and Cabbage Dumplings (水饺, shui jiao) are a classic Northern Chinese boiled dumpling made with a thin, hand-rolled wheat-flour wrapper encasing a seasoned filling of ground pork and finely chopped Tianjin (napa) cabbage, typically flavoured with ginger, sesame oil, soy sauce, and salt. The dumplings are folded by hand into a crescent or pleated shape and cooked by boiling in water until the wrapper becomes tender and slightly translucent while the filling remains juicy. The texture is soft and yielding with a moist, savoury pork-and-cabbage interior. This frozen product from Din Tai Fung Singapore is sold in packs of 8 pieces (approximately 160 g per serving) and is designed to be boiled from frozen. Nutritionally, each 100 g provides approximately 195 kcal, 9.8 g protein, 7.2 g fat, and 22.5 g carbohydrate, with a moderate sodium content of 420 mg. The glycemic index is estimated at 48 (low GI), making the glycemic load per 8-piece serving approximately 17. Clinically, the moderate sodium and saturated fat content warrant attention for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease, while the moderate glycemic load is relevant for Type 2 Diabetes management. The dish is a culturally significant staple in Chinese cuisine, widely consumed across Singapore and the Chinese diaspora, and is associated with family gatherings and festive occasions.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Din Tai Fung dumplings?
How to cook frozen pork and cabbage dumplings
Are Din Tai Fung dumplings halal?

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry for this specific Din Tai Fung frozen product was found in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA Branded Foods. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central data for boiled pork-and-vegetable dumplings and cross-referenced with published literature on Chinese boiled dumplings. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for boiled wheat-flour filled dumplings. Confidence is medium rather than low because the adapted source category is a strong compositional match.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central source entries for boiled pork-and-vegetable dumplings. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source. Trans fat is reported as 0.1 g per 100 g reflecting minor naturally occurring trans fats from pork; this is not an industrially added trans fat. The food is not halal-certified as it contains pork. The 8-piece serving weight of 160 g is an estimate based on typical Din Tai Fung dumpling sizing (~20 g per piece); actual product weight may vary. GI of 48 is an analogous estimate; no direct measurement for this specific product exists. Glycemic load per serving = (48 × 36.0) / 100 = 17.3, where 36.0 g carbohydrate is derived from 22.5 g/100 g × 160 g serving.
